title: FortiSASE Thin Edge Health (Fortinet)
spl:
index=sase sourcetype="fgt_event" subtype IN ("vpn","system") earliest=-24h
| eval down=if(match(lower(msg),"(?i)tunnel.*down|ipsec.*fail|phase[12].*fail"),1,0)
| where down=1
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event by tunnelid, remip, tunneltype
| sort -count
description: FortiSASE thin edges (FortiExtender, FortiGate in SASE mode) connect branches to the nearest FortiSASE PoP. Tunnel state, latency, and failover events determine branch connectivity and SLA compliance.
value: Network operations teams monitor FortiSASE Thin Edge device health — tunnel status, CPU/memory utilization, firmware compliance — ensuring branch sites maintain SASE connectivity and identifying edge devices approaching resource limits before they degrade user experience.
implementation: Map `remip` to branch site via CMDB lookup. Alert on sustained tunnel-down (>15 min). Report on failover frequency and PoP selection patterns.
visualization: Table (down tunnels), Map (branch sites), Line chart (tunnel availability %), Single value (sites currently down).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• FortiSASE Thin Edge (FortiExtender or FortiGate as edge) connectivity events in `index=fortisase` with `sourcetype=fortisase:system` or `sourcetype=fortisase:connectivity`. Key fields: `devname`, `devid`, `tunnel_status` (up/down), `wan_ip`, `wan_status`, `cpu_usage`, `mem_usage`, `uptime`, `firmware_version`.
• FortiSASE Thin Edge devices connect branch sites to the FortiSASE cloud. They are lightweight devices that tunnel all traffic to the nearest FortiSASE PoP for security inspection. Device health directly impacts branch connectivity.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify edge device events:
```spl
index=fortisase sourcetype IN ("fortisase:system","fortisase:connectivity") earliest=-24h
| where isnotnull(devname)
| stats count by devname, tunnel_status
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Edge device health:
```spl
index=fortisase sourcetype IN ("fortisase:system","fortisase:connectivity") earliest=-24h
| dedup devname sortby -_time
| eval health=case(
    tunnel_status="down", "DOWN",
    tonumber(cpu_usage)>80 OR tonumber(mem_usage)>80, "STRESSED",
    1==1, "Healthy"
  )
| table devname, devid, tunnel_status, cpu_usage, mem_usage, firmware_version, uptime, health
| sort health
```

Understanding this SPL: Shows the health of each Thin Edge device. 'DOWN' means the site has no FortiSASE connectivity — traffic either bypasses cloud security or stops. 'STRESSED' means the device is approaching resource limits — may drop packets or degrade performance under peak load.

Firmware compliance:
```spl
index=fortisase sourcetype="fortisase:system" earliest=-24h
| dedup devname sortby -_time
| stats count by firmware_version
| sort firmware_version
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In FortiSASE: Network > Edges. Compare device status.
(b) Reboot a test edge device. Verify the down/up sequence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Edge device status overview. Row 2 — Stressed devices. Row 3 — Firmware versions.
Alert: Immediate when any edge device tunnel goes down.

Runbook:
1. **Tunnel down** — Check WAN link at the site. Power cycle edge device.
2. **CPU/memory stressed** — Site may need a larger device or traffic optimization.
3. **Outdated firmware** — Schedule upgrade during maintenance window.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No edge events** — Device may not be reporting. Check device connectivity.
• **`cpu_usage` always 0** — SNMP or health reporting may not be enabled on the edge device.
grandmaExplanation: Each office has a small device that connects to our cloud security system. We monitor each device to make sure it is running properly and not overloaded, because if a device fails, that entire office loses its security protection and may lose internet access altogether.
hardware: FortiSASE (cloud PoPs), FortiExtender 200F/400F, FortiGate SD-WAN appliances in FortiSASE mode
